title: The Exponentially Varying Sinusoid
summary: This page explains how to sketch an exponentially varying sinusoid of the form Ae^(-at)cos(ω0t + θ) by separately sketching the decaying exponential and the sinusoid, then multiplying them. A specific example x(t) = 4e^(-2t)cos(6t - 60°) is worked through step by step, showing how the exponential envelopes 4e^(-2t) and -4e^(-2t) constrain the amplitude of the sinusoidal component. The method generalizes to any Ke^(-at)cos(ω0t + θ) signal.
